title: Single-Pixel Camera (SPC) with Hadamard structured illumination
source_data_quality_note: The source MD at B_compressive_imaging/026_spc.md contains the widefield-fluorescence template (genesis fan-out artifact). Physics below is extracted from the canonical mine_example/spc.md reference.

E.description: SPC: a DMD (digital micromirror device) imposes a sequence of Hadamard-style binary +/-1 patterns on the incoming optical field, and a single photodetector integrates the pattern-weighted irradiance; m scalar measurements permit recovery of the H*W image when m/n ~ 0.25 and the image is sparse.
E.forward_model: y_k = <phi_k, x> + n_k,  Phi from Hadamard basis at sampling ratio m/n
E.world_state_x: 2D radiance image x in R^n, n = H*W, non-negative, sparse in wavelet/TV basis
E.observation_y: m scalar photodetector readings y in R^m (additive Gaussian)
physical_parameters_theta: Phi_Hadamard: Walsh-Hadamard rows selected by permutation, binary +/-1

W.regime: Hadamard measurement at 25% sampling satisfies RIP for sparse recovery; Gate-1 minimum compression ratio ~5%. Residual-based calibration fails for SPC (undetermined self-consistency); TV-based objective required for gain-drift and illumination-non-uniformity correction.

C.solver_class: FISTA-TV, ISTA-Net, LISTA, or learned deep unrolling
C.error_bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma / sqrt(m) + C2 * ||x - x_s||_1
C.complexity: O(n * log n) per iteration (fast Hadamard transform)
